title: Pass 5.B.1 follow-up: multivariate_normal sample/rsample/log_prob fail-fast on CUDA via fallback.rs (Pattern A)
description:
Pass 5.B.1 (#1103) migrated transforms.rs to device-resident ops (Pattern B). multivariate_normal.rs cannot follow Pattern B in this dispatch because its scalar bodies depend on a local `cholesky_lower` implementation (scalar f64 loop, multivariate_normal.rs:349-373) and there is no GPU cholesky kernel in ferrotorch-core.

The fallback.rs gate is already wired into every MVN method, so on Linux/CPU runs nothing changes. The remaining work is to keep the fallback.rs guards in place and document that full Pattern B migration is blocked on a GPU cholesky (and likely a GPU triangular-solve) kernel. PyTorch parity: torch.distributions.MultivariateNormal on CUDA dispatches to a real CUDA cholesky; we don't yet have one, so Pattern A (loud error) is the right posture for now.

Sites already guarded by check_gpu_fallback_opt_in (Pattern A is in place):
- multivariate_normal.rs:141 MultivariateNormal::sample (eps/loc/scale_tril readback at 151-154, recompose at 171)
- multivariate_normal.rs:180 MultivariateNormal::rsample (same readbacks at 189-192, recompose at 207)
- multivariate_normal.rs:231 MultivariateNormal::log_prob (loc/scale_tril/value readback at 241-243, recompose at 297)
- multivariate_normal.rs:312 MultivariateNormal::entropy (scale_tril at 319, recompose at 333)
- Plus internal cholesky_lower (line 349) and precision-to-cov helper (line 385) — both pure CPU.

Acceptance:
- New finding documents that Pattern A is the production posture for MVN until GPU cholesky/triangular-solve kernels land.
- When ferrotorch-core grows GPU cholesky+trsm, revisit this issue and migrate to Pattern B.
- No code change in this issue beyond verifying the existing fallback.rs guards remain.

Related:
- #982 audit umbrella
- #1103 transforms.rs Pattern B migration (this issue's parent dispatch)
